notes.selected_text: There's just one way to do that—to base them on well-defined, knowledge-based curriculums. There is no other way of making tests fair and productive
notes.note: not just fair and productive, also fewer and better

notes.selected_text: Since a valid reading test probes a student's degree of initiation into the public sphere—a fundamental aim of education—any policy that lowers or neglects to improve test scores in reading is a failed educational policy.
In math, in contrast to reading, American scores for seventeen- year-olds have been stable for many years.
. Thirteen-year-olds have made steady prog- ress in reading, but the scores of seventeen-year-olds have remained flat.
notes.note: this seems important - how does this fit with his theory, why would 13 year olds have made progress?

notes.selected_text: If one had to choose a single measure...
notes.note: extremely important claim, load-bearing. A lot of disagreement here (ref PISA discussions)

notes.selected_text: the testing regimens have clearly helped improve the mechanics of early reading
Younger students can now decode texts with more fluency and accuracy than they did be- fore NCLB.
notes.note: interesting given reading wars and phonics discussion

notes.selected_text: But, alas, those equity gaps begin widening again between ages thirteen and seventeen, when knowledge and vocabulary are decisive
notes.note: aren't knowledge gaps decisive before 13? Also is this him adapting theories to data - do we see this in France/Finland?

notes.selected_text: They now were being made to teach reading instead...
notes.note: What evidence exists for what students actually spend time on in school? What influences this? Tests, principals, curricula, textbooks, technology?

notes.selected_text: Despite their indifference to the school curriculum, the better reading tests are technically reliable and valid...
notes.note: Crucial - need to dig deep into what reading ability means and how it's measured. Goes far beyond Hirsch.

notes.selected_text: But these tests as currently used... consequential validity...
But once decoding has been mastered and fluency attained, relevant knowledge becomes the chief component of reading skill.
State tests in math have been based on specific content standards...
notes.note: Key - expand on. Compare curricula worldwide. Find the iron-man argument for skills-based teaching. What theories and evidence do skills proponents have?

notes.selected_text: The no- tion that skill in finding the main idea can take the place of content is worse than empty; it's actively misleading. There is no reliable main-idea-finding skill.
Willingham and Lovette, Can Reading Comprehension Be Taught?
notes.note: Studies cited in main text are likely load-bearing — need to be featured centrally and processed.

notes.selected_text: Their utility ceases after about ten lessons. Two weeks on comprehension strategies is optimal.
notes.note: Has anyone disagreed or offered evidence against? What is the scholarly discussion?
